$comment: c52 (D-FFI-MATH-INFINITY): ship INFINITY from <math.h> as an f64 float constant. sqlite uses `(double)INFINITY` (sqlite3.c:37393/37423 — a RUNTIME expression) -> S0001 INFINITY undeclared; the integer `constants` surface is integer-only, so a NEW `floatConstants` surface carries it (injected as an f64 named constant whose HIR Ref folds to a +inf literal 0x7FF0000000000000). `(double)INFINITY > 1e308` is true -> exit 42. The release/full-release-like arms ConstFold `+inf > 1e308` to true (the const-eval Float->Float identity cast preserves +inf; only Float->INT casts of inf refuse). RED-ON-DISABLE: remove the math.json floatConstants INFINITY entry -> S0001. ★ INFINITY's value + the +inf bit pattern are verified on the pe RUN (exit 42) + x86_64 disassembly of the .data f64 const. The elf/arm64 RUN legs were COMPILE-ONLY (runOn:[]) from c52 through c86 — the math.h/libm DT_NEEDED gap: math.json's elf library said libc.so.6 while glibc's math symbols live in libm.so.6, so the eagerly-imported sqrt/pow dynsyms failed at LOAD (undefined symbol: sqrt, exit 127). c87 (D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED) closed it (math.json elf library = libm.so.6 + the ELF walker emits one DT_NEEDED per DISTINCT referenced import library), restoring the linux RUN on both arches exactly as the c52 deferral promised. This example still calls NO math function — it pins that a math.h INCLUDE alone (which eagerly imports the whole math.json surface) yields a LOADABLE image on elf.
